items.title: A/B test conversion rate
items.scenario: An e-commerce team runs an A/B test on 10,000 visitors per arm. Variant B shows 520 conversions (5.20%) vs control A at 480 (4.80%). They report a 95% CI for the difference in proportions to decide whether the lift is distinguishable from noise.
items.explanation: Each arm's proportion has sampling uncertainty; the CI for the lift quantifies whether the observed gap could plausibly be zero if there were no real effect.

items.title: Model accuracy on a holdout set
items.scenario: A classifier scores 850/1000 correct on a test set (85% accuracy). Before deploying, the team publishes a 95% Wilson interval for true population accuracy rather than treating 85% as exact.
items.explanation: The holdout is a sample from the deployment population; the CI communicates how much the point estimate might move with more labeled data.

items.title: Average inference latency in production monitoring
items.scenario: Over one hour, 200 requests to an LLM endpoint have mean latency 1.8 s and standard deviation 0.6 s. SREs plot a 95% CI for mean latency alongside the point estimate on the dashboard.
items.explanation: The interval separates a real regression from normal hour-to-hour noise when comparing to last week's baseline.
